action.reasoning: Observation: the 0.25 interpolation between the best 26-epoch checkpoint and degraded 34-epoch checkpoint scored 0.4760, essentially tied but slightly below the 26-epoch best of 0.4763. Analysis: training beyond 26 epochs appears risky, while cheap inference tuning may still improve mIoU. The previous worse inference test combined short_side=768 with flip TTA on an older 8-epoch checkpoint, so it did not isolate moderate higher-resolution no-flip inference on the current best model. Decision: inspect current predict.py, change only the default inference short_side from 512 to 640 with no flip TTA, and submit the best 26-epoch checkpoint to measure whether moderate resolution improves validation mIoU.
action.command:
grep -n "short_side\|flip_tta\|def preprocess\|def worker" predict.py && python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('predict.py')
s=p.read_text()
s=s.replace("ap.add_argument('--short_side', type=int, default=512)", "ap.add_argument('--short_side', type=int, default=640)")
p.write_text(s)
print('patched predict.py short_side default to 640')
PY
